translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Our Lord, You have knowledge of all that we hide and all that we reveal; nothing in heaven or earth is hidden from Allah.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Our Lord, indeed You know what we conceal and what we declare, and nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the heaven.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Lord, You know all that we conceal or reveal. Nothing in the heavens or the earth is hidden from God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O our Lord! Surely Thou knowest what we hide and what we make public, and nothing in the earth nor any thing in heaven is hidden from Allah:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Lord, You have knowledge of all that we hide and all that we reveal: nothing in heaven or on earth is hidden from God.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "O our Lord! truly Thou dost know what we conceal and what we reveal: for nothing whatever is hidden from Allah, whether on earth or in heaven.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Rabbana innaka taAAlamu ma nukhfee wama nuAAlinu wama yakhfa AAala Allahi min shayin fee alardi wala fee alssamai

39.arabic: ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى وَهَبَ لِى عَلَى ٱلْكِبَرِ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى لَسَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: All praise be to God who bestowed on me Ishmael and Isaac in old age. Verily my Lord listens to prayer.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “All praise is to Allah Who gave me Ismail and Ishaq, in my old age; indeed my Lord is the Listener of Prayer.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Praise be to God, who has given me, though I am old, Ishmael and Isaac; surely my Lord hears the petition.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: All praise be unto Allah who hath bestowed upon me, despite old age lsma'il and ls-haq; verily my Lord is the Hearer of supplication.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "All the praises and thanks be to Allah, Who has given me in old age Isma'il (Ishmael) and Ishaque (Isaac). Verily! My Lord is indeed the All-Hearer of invocations.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Praise be to God, Who has given me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac. My Lord is the Hearer of Prayers.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: All praise be to Allah Who, despite my old age, has given me Ishmael and Isaac. Surely my Lord hears all prayers.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "All praise is due to Allah, Who has given me in old age Isma'il and Ishaq. Verily, my Lord is indeed the All-Hearer of invocations."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Praise be to Allah Who hath given me, in my old age, Ishmael and Isaac! Lo! my Lord is indeed the Hearer of Prayer.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: All praise belongs to Allah, who gave me Ishmael and Isaac despite [my] old age. Indeed my Lord hears all supplications.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Praise be to Allah who has given me Ishmael and Isaac in my old age! Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of the supplication.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Praise to Allah, who has granted to me in old age Ishmael and Isaac. Indeed, my Lord is the Hearer of supplication.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It is only God who deserves all praise. I praise Him for His giving me my sons Ishmael and Isaac during my old age. My Lord, certainly, hears all prayers.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Praise be to Allah, Who has given me in old age Ismail and Ishaq; most surely my Lord is the Hearer of prayer:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Praise be to God who has bestowed upon me, despite my old age, Ishmael and Isaac. Surely my Lord is the hearer of prayer.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "Praise be to Allah, Who hath granted unto me in old age Isma'il and Isaac: for truly my Lord is He, the Hearer of Prayer!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alhamdu lillahi allathee wahaba lee AAala alkibari ismaAAeela waishaqa inna rabbee lasameeAAu aldduAAai

40.arabic: رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْنِى مُقِيمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَآءِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Grant, O Lord, that I and my offspring may remain constant in devotion. Grant, O Lord, my supplication.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “O my Lord! Maintain me as one who establishes prayer, and some of my descendants; O our Lord! and accept my prayer.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: My Lord, make me a performer of the prayer, and of my seed. Our Lord, and receive my petition.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: My Lord! make me establisher of prayer and also from my progeny, our Lord! and accept Thou my supplication.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "O my Lord! Make me one who performs As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat), and (also) from my offspring, our Lord! And accept my invocation.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “My Lord, make me one who performs the prayer, and from my offspring. Our Lord, accept my supplication.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: My Lord! Enable me and my offspring to establish Prayer, and do accept, our Lord, this prayer of mine.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "O my Lord! Make me one who performs Salah, and (also) from my offspring, our Lord! And accept my invocation."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: My Lord! Make me to establish proper worship, and some of my posterity (also); our Lord! and accept my prayer.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: My Lord! Make me a maintainer of prayer, and my descendants [as well]. Our Lord, accept my supplication.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: My Lord, make me and my descendants establishers of prayer. Our Lord, accept my prayer.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer, and [many] from my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Lord, make me and my offspring steadfast in prayer and accept our worship.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: My Lord! make me keep up prayer and from my offspring (too), O our Lord, and accept my prayer:

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Lord, grant that I may keep up the prayer, and so may my offspring. My Lord, accept my prayer.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: O my Lord! make me one who establishes regular Prayer, and also (raise such) among my offspring O our Lord! and accept Thou my Prayer.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Rabbi ijAAalnee muqeema alssalati wamin thurriyyatee rabbana wtaqabbal duAAai

41.arabic: رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلْحِسَابُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: O Lord, forgive me, my parents and the faithful on the Day the reckoning is done."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “O our Lord! And forgive me, and my parents, and all the Muslims on the day when the account will be established.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Our Lord, forgive Thou me and my parents, and the believers, upon the day when the reckoning shall come to pass.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Our Lord! forgive me and my parents and the believers on the Day whereon will be set up the reckoning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (all) the believers on the Day when the reckoning will be established."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: “Our Lord, forgive me, and my parents, and the believers, on the Day the Reckoning takes place.”

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents and the believers on the Day when the reckoning will take place."

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and (all) the believers on the Day when the reckoning will be established."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents and believers on the day when the account is cast.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and all the faithful, on the day when the reckoning is held.’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Forgive me, our Lord, and forgive my parents and all the believers on the Day of Reckoning'

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Our Lord, forgive me and my parents and the believers the Day the account is established."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Lord, on the Day of Judgment, forgive me and my parents and all the believers."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: O our Lord! grant me protection and my parents and the believers on the day when the reckoning shall come to pass!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Forgive me, Lord, and forgive my parents and all the believers on the Day of Reckoning."

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "O our Lord! cover (us) with Thy Forgiveness - me, my parents, and (all) Believers, on the Day that the Reckoning will be established!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Rabbana ighfir lee waliwalidayya walilmumineena yawma yaqoomu alhisabu

42.arabic: وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَـٰفِلًا عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ تَشْخَصُ فِيهِ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Think not God is oblivious of the deeds of the wicked. He has only allowed them respite till the Day on which all eyes would stare aghast,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And do not ever assume that Allah is unaware of what the unjust do; He does not give them respite but for a day in which the eyes will become fixed, staring.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Deem not that God is heedless of what the evildoers work; He is only deferring them to a day when eyes shall stare,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And deem not Allah negligent of that which the wrong-doers work: He only deferreth them to a Day whereon the sighthall remain staring.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Consider not that Allah is unaware of that which the Zalimun (polytheists, wrong-doers, etc.) do, but He gives them respite up to a Day when the eyes will stare in horror.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do not ever think that God is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only defers them until a Day when the sights stare.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Do not think Allah is heedless of the evil deeds in which the evil-doers are engaged. He is merely granting them respite until a Day when their eyes shall continue to stare in horror,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Consider not that Allah is unaware of that which the wrongdoers do, but He gives them respite up to a Day when the eyes will stare in horror.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Deem not that Allah is unaware of what the wicked do. He but giveth them a respite till a day when eyes will stare (in terror),

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Do not suppose that Allah is oblivious of what the wrongdoers are doing. He is only granting them respite until the day when the eyes will be glazed.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not think that Allah is unaware of the harmdoers work. He only gives them respite till the Day on which all eyes will stare,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And never think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them for a Day when eyes will stare [in horror].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), do not think that God is unaware of what the unjust people do. He only gives them a respite until the day when the eyes will stare fixedly,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And do not think Allah to be heedless of what the unjust do; He only respites them to a day on which the eyes shall be fixedly open,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Do not think God is unaware of the wrongdoers' actions. He only gives them respite till the Day on which all eyes will stare fixedly in horror.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Think not that Allah doth not heed the deeds of those who do wrong. He but giveth them respite against a Day when the eyes will fixedly stare in horror,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wala tahsabanna Allaha ghafilan AAamma yaAAmalu alththalimoona innama yuakhkhiruhum liyawmin tashkhasu feehi alabsaru

43.arabic: مُهْطِعِينَ مُقْنِعِى رُءُوسِهِمْ لَا يَرْتَدُّ إِلَيْهِمْ طَرْفُهُمْ ۖ وَأَفْـِٔدَتُهُمْ هَوَآءٌ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: (And) they would hasten forward, heads lifted upwards, gazes fixed, and emptied out their hearts.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They shall come out running in bewilderment, with their heads raised, their gaze not returning to them; and their hearts will not have any calm.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: when they shall run with necks outstretched and heads erect, their glances never returned on themselves, their hearts void.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They hastening forward, their heads upraised, their look returning not unto them and their hearts void.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: (They will be) hastening forward with necks outstretched, their heads raised up (towards the sky), their gaze returning not towards them and their hearts empty (from thinking because of extreme fear).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Their necks outstretched, their heads upraised, their gaze unblinking, their hearts void.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: when they shall keep pressing ahead in haste, their heads lifted up, their gaze directed forward, unable to look away from what they behold, their hearts utterly void.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: (They will be) hastening forward with necks outstretched, their heads raised up (towards the sky), their gaze returning not towards them and their hearts empty.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: As they come hurrying on in fear, their heads upraised, their gaze returning not to them, and their hearts as air.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Scrambling with their heads upturned, there will be a fixed gaze in their eyes, and their hearts will be vacant.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: when they shall run with their necks outstretched and heads erect, their glances never return to themselves, their hearts are empty.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Racing ahead, their heads raised up, their glance does not come back to them, and their hearts are void.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: when people will hurry in fright, their heads raised, their eyes unable to look around, and their hearts stunned due to the confusion (which will prevail on that Day).

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Hastening forward, their heads upraised, their eyes not reverting to them and their hearts vacant.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They will hurry on in fright, their heads lifted up, their gaze directed forward, their minds utterly void.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: They running forward with necks outstretched, their heads uplifted, their gaze returning not towards them, and their hearts a (gaping) void!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: MuhtiAAeena muqniAAee ruoosihim la yartaddu ilayhim tarfuhum waafidatuhum hawaon

44.arabic: وَأَنذِرِ ٱلنَّاسَ يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ فَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ أَخِّرْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ نُّجِبْ دَعْوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ ٱلرُّسُلَ ۗ أَوَلَمْ تَكُونُوٓا۟ أَقْسَمْتُم مِّن قَبْلُ مَا لَكُم مِّن زَوَالٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Warn the people of that Day when the punishment would be inflicted upon them. Then will the wicked say: "O our Lord, give us respite a while more. We shall heed Your call and follow the apostles." (But they will be told,): "Are you not those who used to swear: 'There is no reverse for us?'

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And warn the people of a day when the punishment will come upon them, therefore the unjust will say, “O our Lord! Give us respite for a little while – for us to obey Your call and follow the Noble Messengers”; (It will be said) “So had you not sworn before that, ‘We have not to move to any place else from the earth’?”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And warn mankind of the day when the chastisement comes on them, And those who did evil shall say, 'Our Lord, defer us to a near term, and we will answer Thy call, and follow the Messengers.' 'Ah, but did you not swear aforetime there should be no removing for you?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And warn thou the mankind of the Day whereon the torment will come unto them; then those who have done wrong shall say: our Lord! defer us to a term near at hand we will answer Thy call and we will follow the apostles. Were ye not wont to swear afore that for you there was to be no decline?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And warn (O Muhammad SAW) mankind of the Day when the torment will come unto them; then the wrong-doers will say: "Our Lord! Respite us for a little while, we will answer Your Call and follow the Messengers!" (It will be said): "Had you not sworn aforetime that you would not leave (the world for the Hereafter).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And warn mankind of the Day when the punishment will come upon them, and the wicked will say, “Our Lord, defer us for a little while, and we will answer Your call and follow the messengers.” Did you not swear before that there will be no passing away for you?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Muhammad), warn mankind of the Day when a severe chastisement shall overtake them, and the wrong-doers will say: "Our Lord, grant us respite for a short while; we shall respond to Your call and will follow Your Messengers." (But they will be clearly told): "Are you not the same who swore earlier that they shall never suffer decline?"

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And warn mankind of the Day when the torment will come unto them; then the wrongdoers will say: "Our Lord! Respite us for a little while, we will answer Your call and follow the Messengers!" (It will be said:) "Had you not sworn aforetime that you would not leave (the world for the Hereafter)."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And warn mankind of a day when the doom will come upon them, and those who did wrong will say: Our Lord! Reprieve us for a little while. We will obey Thy call and will follow the messengers. (It will be answered): Did ye not swear before that there would be no end for you?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Warn the people of the day when the punishment will overtake them, whereat the wrongdoers will say, ‘Our Lord! Respite us for a short time so that we may respond to Your call and follow the apostles.’ [They will be told,] ‘Did you not use to swear earlier that there would be no reverse for you,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Warn mankind of the Day when the punishment will overtake them, when the evildoers will say: 'Our Lord, grant us respite for awhile, we will answer Your call, and follow the Messengers' Did you not once swear that you would never cease?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And, [O Muhammad], warn the people of a Day when the punishment will come to them and those who did wrong will say, "Our Lord, delay us for a short term; we will answer Your call and follow the messengers." [But it will be said], "Had you not sworn, before, that for you there would be no cessation?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), warn the people of the day when torment will approach them and the unjust will say, "Lord, give us respite for a little time so that we may answer your call and follow the Messengers." (The answer to their prayer will be), "Did you not swear before that you would never perish?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And warn people of the day when the chastisement shall come to them, then those who were unjust will say: O our Lord! respite us to a near term, (so) we shall respond to Thy call and follow the apostles. What! did you not swear before (that) there will be no passing away for you!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Warn men of the Day when the punishment will come upon them, and when the wrongdoers will say, "Our Lord, grant us respite for a short while. We will respond to Your call and will follow the messengers." [But God will answer], are you not those who swore that you would never suffer any decline?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So warn mankind of the Day when the Wrath will reach them: then will the wrong-doers say: "Our Lord! respite us (if only) for a short term: we will answer Thy call, and follow the messengers!" "What! were ye not wont to swear aforetime that ye should suffer no decline?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waanthiri alnnasa yawma yateehimu alAAathabu fayaqoolu allatheena thalamoo rabbana akhkhirna ila ajalin qareebin nujib daAAwataka wanattabiAAi alrrusula awalam takoonoo aqsamtum min qablu ma lakum min zawalin

45.arabic: وَسَكَنتُمْ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنَا بِهِمْ وَضَرَبْنَا لَكُمُ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet you dwelt in the dwellings of those who had exceeded themselves, and it was evident to you how We had dealt with them; and We held out examples before you."

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: “And you dwelt in the houses of those who had wronged themselves and it became very clear to you how We had dealt with them, and We illustrated several examples for you.”

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And you dwelt in the dwelling-places of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We did with them, and how We struck similitudes for you.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And ye dwell in the dwellings of those who had wronged themselves and it had become manifest unto you in what wise We had dealt with them, and We had propounded for you similitudes.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: "And you dwelt in the dwellings of men who wronged themselves, and it was clear to you how We had dealt with them. And We put forth (many) parables for you."

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And you inhabited the homes of those who wronged themselves, and it became clear to you how We dealt with them, and We cited for you the examples.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: You said so even though you had lived in the dwellings of those who had wronged themselves (by sinning), and you were aware how We dealt with them, and We had even explained to you all this by giving examples.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: "And you dwelt in the dwellings of men who wronged themselves, and it was clear to you how We dealt with them. And We put forth (many) parables for you."

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And (have ye not) dwelt in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves (of old) and (hath it not) become plain to you how We dealt with them and made examples for you?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: while you dwelt in the dwellings of those who had wronged themselves [before], and it had been made clear to you how We had dealt with them [before you], and We had [also] cited examples for you?’

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: You lived in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it was clear to you how We dealt with them, and how We struck parables for you.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you lived among the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it had become clear to you how We dealt with them. And We presented for you [many] examples."

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: You lived in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, even though it was made clear to you how We dealt with them. We also showed you examples."

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you dwell in the abodes of those who were unjust to themselves, and it is clear to you how We dealt with them and We have made (them) examples to you.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: You lived in the dwellings of those who wronged themselves, and it was made clear to you how We had dealt with them and We gave you many examples.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: "And ye dwelt in the dwellings of men who wronged their own souls; ye were clearly shown how We dealt with them; and We put forth (many) parables in your behoof!"

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wasakantum fee masakini allatheena thalamoo anfusahum watabayyana lakum kayfa faAAalna bihim wadarabna lakumu alamthala

46.arabic: وَقَدْ مَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرَهُمْ وَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَكْرُهُمْ وَإِن كَانَ مَكْرُهُمْ لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ ٱلْجِبَالُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Still they are plotting their plots, but evident are their plots to God, even though they are so adroit as to make the mountains move.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed they carried out their scheme, and their scheming is within Allah’s control; and their scheme was not such that could move these mountains*. (* The revelations / signs of Allah.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They devised their devising, and their devising is known to God; though their devising were such as to remove mountains.'

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of a surety theys Plotted their plot, and with Allah was their plot, though their plot was such as to remove thereby mountains.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Indeed, they planned their plot, and their plot was with Allah, though their plot was a great (one, still) it would never be able to remove the mountains (real mountains or the Islamic law) from their places (as it is of no importance) [Tafsir Ibn Kathir, Vol. 2, Page 597]. [It is said by some interpreters regarding this Verse that the Quraish pagans plotted against Prophet Muhammad SAW to kill him but they failed and were unable to carry out their plot which they plotted].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They planned their plans, but their plans are known to God, even if their plans can eliminate mountains.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed the unbelievers contrived their plan, but it is in Allah's power to nullify their plan, even though their plans were such that would move even mountains.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Indeed, they planned their plot, and their plot was with Allah, though their plot was not such as to remove the mountains from their places.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Verily they have plotted their plot, and their plot is with Allah, though their plot were one whereby the mountains should be moved.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They certainly devised their plots, but their plots are known to Allah, and their plots are not such as to dislodge the mountains.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They plotted their plots, but their plots are (known) to Allah, even though their plots were such as to move mountains.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they had planned their plan, but with Allah is [recorded] their plan, even if their plan had been [sufficient] to do away with the mountains.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They devised evil plans which were so sinful that even the mountains could not endure them. All these were known to God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they have indeed planned their plan, but their plan is with Allah, though their plan was such that the mountains should pass away thereby.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They hatched their plots; but these plots were all within God's knowledge. Though their plots were such as to shake the mountains, God will bring their plots to nothing.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Mighty indeed were the plots which they made, but their plots were (well) within the sight of Allah, even though they were such as to shake the hills!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waqad makaroo makrahum waAAinda Allahi makruhum wain kana makruhum litazoola minhu aljibalu

47.arabic: فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلَهُۥٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Think not that God would go back on His promise (made) to the apostles. Indeed God is mighty, the Lord of retribution.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So do not ever assume that Allah will not fulfil His promise to His Noble Messengers; indeed Allah is the Dominant, the Avenger.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So do not deem that God will fail in His promise to His Messengers; surely God is All-mighty, Vengeful.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: So deem not thouthat Allah is going to fail His promise to His apostles; verily Allah is Mighty, Lord of vengeance

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So think not that Allah will fail to keep His Promise to His Messengers. Certainly, Allah is All-Mighty, - All-Able of Retribution.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Do not ever think that God will break His promise to His messengers. God is Strong, Able to Avenge.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So, do not think, (O Prophet), that Allah will go back upon His promise to His Messengers. Surely Allah is Mighty, Lord of retribution.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So think not that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His Messengers. Certainly, Allah is All-Mighty, All-Able of Retribution.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: So think not that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His messengers. Lo! Allah is Mighty, Able to Requite (the wrong).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So do not suppose that Allah will break His promise to His apostles. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, avenger.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Do not think that Allah will break the promise He gave to His Messengers. Mighty is Allah, and Vengeful.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So never think that Allah will fail in His promise to His messengers. Indeed, Allah is Exalted in Might and Owner of Retribution.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: You must not even think that God will disregard His promise to His messengers. God is Majestic and Revengeful.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore do not think Allah (to be one) failing in His promise to His apostles; surely Allah is Mighty, the Lord of Retribution.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Never think that God will fail in His promise to His messengers. God is mighty and capable of retribution.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Never think that Allah would fail his messengers in His promise: for Allah is Exalted in power, - the Lord of Retribution.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fala tahsabanna Allaha mukhlifa waAAdihi rusulahu inna Allaha AAazeezun thoo intiqamin

48.arabic: يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ ٱلْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ ۖ وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلْوَٰحِدِ ٱلْقَهَّارِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: The day when the earth will he replaced by some other than the earth, as will be the skies, and every one will appear before God the one and omnipotent,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: On the day when the earth will be changed to other than this earth, and the heavens – and they will all come forth standing before Allah, the One, the Dominant above all.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Upon the day the earth shall be changed to other than the earth, and the heavens and they sally forth unto God, the One, the Omnipotent.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: On the Day whereon the earth will be changed into other than the earth, and the heavens also; and all creatures will appear unto Allah, the One, the Subduer.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will be the heavens, and they (all creatures) will appear before Allah, the One, the Irresistible.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: On the Day when the earth is changed into another earth, and the heavens, and they will emerge before God, the One, the Irresistible.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (Do warn them of the) Day when the heavens and the earth shall be altogether changed; when all will appear fully exposed before Allah, the One, the Irresistible!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: On the Day when the earth will be changed to another earth and so will be the heavens, and they (all creatures) will appear before Allah, the One, the Irresistible.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: On the day when the earth will be changed to other than the earth, and the heavens (also will be changed) and they will come forth unto Allah, the One, the Almighty,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The day when the earth is turned into another earth and the heavens [as well], and they are presented before Allah, the One, the All-paramount

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: On the Day when the earth is changed into other than the earth and the heavens, all shall come before Allah, the One, the Conqueror.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: [It will be] on the Day the earth will be replaced by another earth, and the heavens [as well], and all creatures will come out before Allah, the One, the Prevailing.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: On the day when the earth and the heavens will be replaced by another earth and heavens and everyone will be brought before the One Almighty God,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: On the day when the earth shall be changed into a different earth, and the heavens (as well), and they shall come forth before Allah, the One, the Supreme.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: On the Day when the earth shall be changed into another earth, as shall be the heavens, they will all appear before God, the One, the Most Supreme.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: One day the earth will be changed to a different earth, and so will be the heavens, and (men) will be marshalled forth, before Allah, the One, the Irresistible;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Yawma tubaddalu alardu ghayra alardi waalssamawatu wabarazoo lillahi alwahidi alqahhari

49.arabic: وَتَرَى ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُّقَرَّنِينَ فِى ٱلْأَصْفَادِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: You will see the wicked on that day bound together in chains.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And on that day you will see the guilty linked together in chains.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And thou shalt see the sinners that day coupled in fetters,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And thou wilt see the guilty on that Day bound together in fetters.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And you will see the Mujrimun (criminals, disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism, polytheists, disobedient to Allah, etc.) that Day bound together in fetters; [Muqarranun in fetters; mean:- with their hands and feet tied to their necks with chains.]

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: On that Day, you will see the sinners bound together in chains.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: On that Day you shall see the guilty ones secured in chains;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And you will see the criminals that Day, Muqarranun (bound together) in fetters.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Thou wilt see the guilty on that day linked together in chains,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: —on that day you will see the guilty bound together in chains,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: On that Day, you shall see the sinners coupled together in fetters;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And you will see the criminals that Day bound together in shackles,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: you will see the guilty ones bound in chains,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And you will see the guilty on that day linked together in chains.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: On that Day you shall see the guilty bound in chains,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And thou wilt see the sinners that day bound together in fetters;-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Watara almujrimeena yawmaithin muqarraneena fee alasfadi

50.arabic: سَرَابِيلُهُم مِّن قَطِرَانٍ وَتَغْشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Of molten pitch shall be their garments, their faces covered with flames,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Their cloaks will be of pitch and fire will cover their faces.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: of pitch their shirts, their faces enveloped by the Fire,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Their trouser hell be of pitch, and the Fire shall cover their faces.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Their garments will be of pitch, and fire will cover their faces.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Their garments made of tar, and the Fire covering their faces.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: their garments shall be black as if made out of pitch, and flames of the Fire shall cover their faces

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Their garments will be of Qatiran (tar), and fire will cover their faces.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Their raiment of pitch, and the Fire covering their faces,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: their garments made of pitch, and the Fire covering their faces,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: their garments shall be of melted tar, and their faces enveloped by the Fire.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Their garments of liquid pitch and their faces covered by the Fire.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: with garments of pitch and faces covered by fire.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Their shirts made of pitch and the fire covering their faces

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: their garments shall be of pitch and the fire shall envelop their faces.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Their garments of liquid pitch, and their faces covered with Fire;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Sarabeeluhum min qatranin wataghsha wujoohahumu alnnaru

51.arabic: لِيَجْزِىَ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That God may reward each soul for its deeds. Indeed God is swift at reckoning!

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: In order that Allah may repay each soul what it had earned; indeed Allah spends no time in judging the account.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: that God may recompense every soul for its earnings; surely God is swift at the reckoning.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That Allah may requite each soul according to that which he hath earned: verily Allah is swift in reckoning.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That Allah may requite each person according to what he has earned. Truly, Allah is Swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: That God may repay each soul according to what it has earned. God is Quick in reckoning.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: so that Allah may requite each person for his deeds. Allah is swift in reckoning.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That Allah may requite each person according to what he has earned. Truly, Allah is swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That Allah may repay each soul what it hath earned. Lo! Allah is swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: so that Allah may reward every soul for what it has earned. Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Allah will recompense each soul according to its earnings. Swift is the Reckoning of Allah.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So that Allah will recompense every soul for what it earned. Indeed, Allah is swift in account.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is how God will recompense each soul for its deeds. God's reckoning is swift.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That Allah may requite each soul (according to) what it has earned; surely Allah is swift in reckoning.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: God will requite each soul according to its deeds. Swift is God's reckoning.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: That Allah may requite each soul according to its deserts; and verily Allah is swift in calling to account.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Liyajziya Allahu kulla nafsin ma kasabat inna Allaha sareeAAu alhisabi

52.arabic: هَـٰذَا بَلَـٰغٌ لِّلنَّاسِ وَلِيُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَلِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is a message for mankind that they may take a warning from it, and may know that He is the one and only God, and that men of wisdom may reflect.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is the message to be conveyed to all mankind – and in order to warn them with it, and for them to know that He is the only One God, and for men of understanding to heed advice.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: This is a Message to be delivered to mankind that they may be warned by it, and that they may know that He is One God, and that all possessed of minds may remember.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: This is a preaching for mankind, and that they may be warned thereby, and that they may know that there is only one God, and that the men of understanding may be admonished.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: This (Quran) is a Message for mankind (and a clear proof against them), in order that they may be warned thereby, and that they may know that He is the only One Ilah (God - Allah) - (none has the right to be worshipped but Allah), and that men of understanding may take heed.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: This is a proclamation for mankind, that they may be warned thereby, and know that He is One God, and that people of understanding may remember.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is a proclamation for all mankind that they may be warned by it, and that they may know that their God is none but the One True God, and that men of understanding may take heed.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: This (Qur'an) is a Message for mankind (and a clear proof against them), in order that they may be warned thereby, and that they may know that He is the only One God and that men of understanding may take heed.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: This is a clear message for mankind in order that they may be warned thereby, and that they may know that He is only One Allah, and that men of understanding may take heed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: This is a proclamation for mankind, so that they may be warned thereby and know that He is indeed the One God, and those who possess intellect may take admonition.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: This is a message to be delivered to mankind so that they may be warned by it, and know that He is (Allah) One God, and so all those possessed of minds may remember.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: This [Qur'an] is notification for the people that they may be warned thereby and that they may know that He is but one God and that those of understanding will be reminded.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is an admonition for the people that they will be warned and know that He is the only God, and so that the people of understanding may take heed.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This is a sufficient exposition for the people and that they may be warned thereby, and that they may know that He is One Allah and that those possessed of understanding may mind.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: This is a message for mankind. Let them take warning from it and know that He is but one God. Let those possessed of understanding may take heed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Here is a Message for mankind: Let them take warning therefrom, and let them know that He is (no other than) One Allah: let men of understanding take heed.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Hatha balaghun lilnnasi waliyuntharoo bihi waliyaAAlamoo annama huwa ilahun wahidun waliyaththakkara oloo alalbabi
